tier_justification: irreversible/outward surface — this PR IS the enforcement mechanism for external sends (Slack/Calendar/Gmail); tier 2 applies independently of loop-level tier and is not downgradable even if split across multiple PRs, per pr-eval-refs/wu2-send-gate.md.

evals.assert: A WELL-FORMED send request (valid tool_name in GATED_TOOL_NAMES, valid schema-conforming tool_input) with NO operator approval action taken is DENIED by the gate hook.
evals.cmd: node --test gate/sendGate.test.ts 2>&1 | grep -i 'no approval\|never resolves' — assert the corresponding test case passes (permissionDecision === 'deny')
evals.negative_control: Temporarily stub the ApprovalSurface to auto-resolve 'approve' with no owner action -> the same test must now report ALLOW, proving the harness can detect an allow outcome and isn't hard-coded to always report deny.

evals.assert: Approval granted for tool_input X; gate invoked with tampered tool_input Y (same tool_name, different content) reusing X's approval -> DENIED (hash binding prevents cross-input reuse).
evals.cmd: node --test gate/sendGate.test.ts 2>&1 | grep -i tamper — assert the tamper-detection test case passes (deny on hash mismatch)
evals.negative_control: Invoke the gate with the SAME input X the approval was granted for (no tampering) -> must ALLOW, proving the test isn't failing to allow anything and specifically catches the tamper case.

evals.assert: Replay of an already-consumed approval (same hash, second gate invocation) is DENIED (one-shot).
evals.cmd: node --test gate/sendGate.test.ts 2>&1 | grep -i 'one-shot\|replay\|consumed' — assert the replay test case passes
evals.negative_control: Run only the FIRST gate call for hash H (no replay) -> must ALLOW, proving the harness isn't failing every call regardless of replay.

evals.assert: A gate callback that THROWS mid-approval (during hash computation, surface dispatch, or audit-log write) results in DENY, not allow-through (fail-closed under exception).
evals.cmd: node --test gate/sendGate.test.ts 2>&1 | grep -i throw — assert the throwing-surface test case resolves permissionDecision==='deny', never absent/undefined
evals.negative_control: Run the same test with a NON-throwing surface performing the identical decision path -> must resolve normally (allow or deny per its actual decision, not universally deny), proving the throw-path is what's being tested.

evals.assert: A Bash tool call whose command matches a known send-API pattern (curl to slack.com/api/chat.postMessage, api.telegram.org/bot.../sendMessage, googleapis.com gmail messages/send or calendar events POST) is DENIED with a redirect-to-MCP-tool message, independent of GATED_TOOL_NAMES membership.
evals.cmd: node --test gate/bashPatterns.test.ts && node --test gate/sendGate.test.ts 2>&1 | grep -i 'bash\|redirect' — assert matchesBashSendPattern returns true for the 4 named send-endpoint commands and the integration test asserts the redirect message
evals.negative_control: Run matchesBashSendPattern against a non-send Bash command ('ls -la' or a read-only Slack API call like users.info) -> must return false / must NOT deny.

evals.assert: A terminal-approved send (owner types 'y') passes the gate and the tool call proceeds (positive control).
evals.cmd: node --test gate/sendGate.test.ts 2>&1 | grep -i terminal — terminal.ts ApprovalSurface stubbed with a scripted 'approve' response wired through sendGate.ts, asserting permissionDecision==='allow'
evals.negative_control: Same setup with a scripted 'deny' response -> must resolve permissionDecision==='deny', proving the terminal surface's result is actually read, not ignored in favor of a hardcoded allow.

evals.assert: A pending approval produces a queue file at ~/.claude/coderails-dashboard/queue/<hash>.json in the frozen QueueFileEntry seam format (hash, toolName, toolInput, createdAt, status).
evals.cmd: node --test gate/surfaces/queue.test.ts (temp override dir, never the real path): write a pending entry, read back the file, assert all 5 QueueFileEntry fields present with correct types
evals.negative_control: Assert against a deliberately WRONG field name (e.g. 'tool_name' snake_case instead of 'toolName') -> must fail, proving the test checks exact field names rather than trivially passing on any JSON file.

evals.assert: An append-only JSONL audit row is written for every gate attempt AND every decision (approved/denied/timeout), across all three surfaces and the Bash-pattern path.
evals.cmd: node --test gate/auditLog.test.ts && node --test gate/sendGate.test.ts 2>&1 | grep -i audit — run one full gate cycle (attempt -> decision) and assert the audit log file gained exactly 2 new parseable JSON lines with event in {attempt, decision}
evals.negative_control: Assert the log does NOT grow when a non-gated tool_name passes through the hook (the hook returns {} early, no audit row) -> proving the count isn't just incrementing on every hook invocation regardless of relevance.

evals.assert: The full gate/ module type-checks cleanly with no new TypeScript errors introduced by this PR.
evals.negative_control: Introduce a deliberate type error (e.g. wrong return type on canonicalise) -> npm run typecheck must exit non-zero, proving the check isn't vacuously green.
